question_text: A spark occurs when you touch a metal object after walking across a carpeted floor. Which of the following best explains why earthing a charged object prevents sparks?

options.label: A
options.text: Earthing allows the static charge to flow safely to the ground instead of building up on the object

options.label: B
options.text: Earthing creates an insulator that prevents any charge from moving

options.label: C
options.text: Earthing removes all electrons from the object permanently

options.label: D
options.text: Earthing reduces the temperature of the charged object so sparks cannot form

correct_answer: A) Earthing allows the static charge to flow safely to the ground instead of building up on the object
explanation: Earthing provides a safe path for built-up static charge to escape to the ground gradually, preventing the sudden discharge that causes sparks. Without earthing, charge builds up until it suddenly jumps across the gap, creating a spark.
